suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit naming conventions for data structures (e.g., pcb_* for PCB structures). Reviewers observed workers using conventions without clear guidance.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: lecture10-vm-examples worker used pcb_* naming in process-context-demo without explicit convention in AGENTS.md

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The content-worker skill should document how to handle sourceRefs for generated content - whether empty arrays are acceptable or if provenance documentation should be added.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: lecture10-assessments: All 23 questions have sourceRefs: [] but AGENTS.md states 'Every content object must preserve provenance metadata'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align assertion ID naming between validation-contract.md (VAL-CONTENT-*, VAL-QUEST-*) and validate-content.ts (VAL-BANK-*). The mismatch could cause confusion when validating.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: lecture10-assessments reviewer noted validation contract uses VAL-CONTENT-009 and VAL-QUEST-001, but script uses VAL-BANK-*

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker transcript entry not found in worker-transcripts.jsonl for session 24718d92
rejectedObservations.reason: The transcript system is optional and the handoff file was complete with all necessary information
This appears to be intentional architecture for bundling - the src/ copy is likely for Vite to include in the bundle while data/processed/ is the canonical source
